reforms.description: The government has significantly increased spending on education to enhance human capital formation, committing ISK60 million to establish the Education and Training Service Centre, which aims to improve educational outcomes and support training for the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in education and training enhances the skills of the workforce, leading to improved productivity and economic diversification.
reforms.status_evidence: The increased spending and establishment of the service centre have been confirmed as operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in education spending and the establishment of a dedicated service centre represent a structural change in human capital policy.
reforms.source_quote: Recognising the importance of human capital formation for Iceland’s ability to diversify and its future economic performance, the government has considerably raised spending on education in recent years.

reforms.description: The government is planning reforms to shorten upper-secondary education to improve educational outcomes and reduce drop-out rates, which are comparatively high in Iceland.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Shortening upper-secondary education is expected to enhance educational outcomes and reduce drop-out rates, thereby improving the overall skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is a planned change rather than an implemented one, indicating a narrower scope.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning appropriate reforms (including the shortening of upper-secondary education) to try to tackle this problem.
